lesson: A dead machine in a hot, enclosed corner with a popped thermal cutout button just needs a reset — but fix the airflow or it trips again. It is not a compressor fault.
symptoms: Machine completely dead — no display, no response.
Machine is in a warm, enclosed corner of the kitchen.

tests.temp-probe: Rear of machine hot to touch. Compressor motor body very warm.
tests.inspect-beater: Thermal cutout button on the compressor motor has physically popped out. Reset it: machine powers straight back on.

flavour: Machine was fine. Machine was hot. Machine is fine again.
authenticityNote: Thermal cutouts on compressor motors trip when ventilation is blocked by the machine's location. Resetting without improving airflow means it trips again — find and fix the root cause before leaving.
